page: Amazon Web Services r6g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by custom AWS Graviton2 processors built on the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz, with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type, and network performance is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across memory-intensive operations and multi-core processing. The instance achieves an average multi-core CPU score of 12132.45 in stress-ng and 5463.0 in Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, reaching 473.1 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 126.06 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 961.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 5216.77. Cryptographic and compression tasks show lower efficiency, with single-threaded gzip compression at 20.31 GB/sec and a Passmark CPU encryption score of 1090.03.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high memory density and solid multi-threaded throughput at a lower resource-to-cost ratio compared to traditional x86 architectures. Database operations perform reliably, with Redis SET operations reaching 9.91 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring 5125.24. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as open-source databases, in-memory caches, real-time big data analytics, and general-purpose web serving. It is less suited for heavy single-threaded compute tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 processors. It features 8 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network connection managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results show strong memory bandwidth, with cached small-block reads reaching 473.1 GB/sec, and steady multi-core performance, though single-core compute and cryptographic speeds are relatively weak. Qualitatively, the ARM64 architecture offers high memory density and efficient resource allocation for memory-bound tasks. This instance is best suited for running in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, data analytics, and memory-intensive web applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps baseline network, ideal for in-memory databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 8 dedicated cores and 64 GB RAM, designed for database and caching workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized virtual server delivering high memory density and efficient multi-core processing for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 8 dedicated AWS Graviton2 ARM64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Delivers 473.1 GB/sec cached memory read bandwidth
Features a 2 Gbps baseline network connection via AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and data analytics
